blockers: Archive extraction failures are converted back to NoAttestations, allowing callers to downgrade instead of failing closed.

The verifier samples only the lexicographically first inner file; if that file is unattested, it returns NoAttestations without checking later files, so mixed-coverage archives can bypass the strict every-regular-file policy.
overall_reasoning: The agent investigated both install-time and lock-time GitHub verification paths, identified the relevant archive utilities, and asked a useful trust-boundary clarification. The answer explicitly required every regular file to be attested and all extraction errors to propagate fail-closed. The patch preserves archive-first verification and limits the change to the GitHub backend, but then contradicts that answer in two security-critical ways: extraction errors are deliberately downgraded to the original NoAttestations result, and only one deterministic member is probed before an unattested probe causes fallback without examining the rest. A mixed archive with an unattested first file and attested later files therefore is not proven fully covered, while downstream fallback remains possible. The repaired semantic verifier rejects the unchanged candidate specifically for the extraction-error downgrade; inspection confirms the independent partial-coverage bypass. The added tests are extensive and the related suite and clippy were run, but one test explicitly blesses the unsafe first-file shortcut and no test enforces the requested extraction-error behavior. The result is not mergeable for this security-sensitive task.

rating_rationales.code_clarity: The helper boundaries and comments are readable, but roughly 660 added lines and extensive policy commentary make the two-call-site change substantially more complex than necessary.
rating_rationales.repository_fit: The implementation reuses the existing GitHub bridge, archive utilities, TempDir, and backend call paths, but performs repeated per-file GitHub verification at the application layer rather than extending the existing multi-subject SLSA verification primitive.
rating_rationales.scope_discipline: Only the GitHub backend and its attestation bridge are touched as clarified, with no unrelated edits, though the implementation and test volume are disproportionate.
rating_rationales.security_and_safety: Archive-first ordering and non-NoAttestations error propagation are sound, but extraction-error downgrade and one-file sampling violate the explicitly selected fail-closed trust policy.
rating_rationales.test_quality: The agent added focused tar/zip, symlink, lifetime, ordering, and result-classification tests and ran cargo check, clippy, focused tests, and the full binary suite. However, tests encode the unsafe probe fallthrough and omit the requested extraction-error fail-closed case.

strengths: Traced the GitHub attestation bridge plus install-time and lock-time consumers before editing.
Asked a consequential, well-framed clarification and selected strict regular-file coverage with GitHub-only scope.
Preserved archive-blob verification as the first attempt and propagated non-NoAttestations verification failures.
Added broad focused tests and ran formatting, clippy, related modules, and the full binary unit suite.
summary: Strong investigation, clarification, and test effort, but two explicit fail-open paths make the security-sensitive archive fallback unmergeable.
